title: Real World Table Tennis
description: This dataset comprises multimodal neurophysiological and kinematic recordings from 25 participants engaged in real-world table tennis gameplay. The collection includes high-density dual-layer electroencephalography, cervical electromyography, inertial measurement unit acceleration data, structural MRI, and synchronized video recordings. Participants completed 60 minutes of table tennis play against both a ball machine and human opponent, with 10 minutes of standing baseline recording. Processed data include independent component analysis decomposition and dipole modeling for EEG signals.
methods_description: Data were collected from 25 participants performing 60 minutes of table tennis play (ball machine and human opponent) plus 10 minutes of standing baseline. Recordings included high-density dual-layer EEG, neck EMG, IMU acceleration, T1 structural MRI, and synchronized video. EEG data underwent independent component analysis decomposition and dipole modeling. Raw data are available in the sourcedata folder.
